summary: Deterministic testing with TestScheduler, virtual time, recorded notifications, and controlled subscription windows.
recommendedPatterns: Use TestScheduler to control time-dependent operators such as debounce, delay, retry, and timeout.
Assert recorded notifications and subscription windows explicitly.
Prefer deterministic virtual time to wall-clock sleeps in tests.
avoidPatterns: Do not test reactive timing with Thread.Sleep or Task.Delay.
Do not rely on ambient timers when virtual time can make the behavior deterministic.
setupSteps: Create a TestScheduler and test observer.
Advance virtual time and assert recorded messages.
commonPitfalls: Forgetting to control the scheduler for the operator under test.
Asserting only values while ignoring completion or error notifications.
